heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 17 April 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 8.9°C) and outside temperature (down 8.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 13%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 16356 lux) and cloud levels (up 1248 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 13272 lux), outside temperature (up 10.9°C), and cloud levels (up 574 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1248.1m lower than yesterday, 317.2m lower than the past week, 573.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1100m minimum and 4850m maximum, with an average of 1547.0m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1864.2m and 2120m.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; subzero temperatures are forecasted. Also, little or no wind. Chance of light rain, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and it’s dark. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-1.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
